steps.title: Identify the contrast
steps.content: The decisive phrase is “though,” which introduces a contrast between focusing on Brooks's immediate surroundings versus her broad literary influences. This pivotal word signals that the correct answer must accommodate this transition appropriately.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content: When though is used as a parenthetical interrupter between an independent clause and a participial phrase that modifies it, it must be set off by commas on both sides: surroundings, though, considering.... Choice A follows this pattern. B inserts a period mid-sentence, breaking the participial phrase from its clause; C uses a semicolon where a comma is required; D places a semicolon after though instead of a comma. That matches choice A — A is correct.
